item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I honestly couldn't even tell this was a corpse con entry, Well done! A great read with a lesson that doesn't bash you over the head what more can you ask for +1

item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded: They weren't an embarrassment at all! A challenge maybe, but I enjoy challenges. Also I'm very glad that you turned out to be the co-author. What are the chances?

item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded: It is I who wrote that embarrassment of conprocs. Surprised by how well you were able to work with them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked it as a draft, and I still like it now. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Such weird conprocs you received, but you definitely pulled an interesting anomaly out of it. Aside from the conversation sounding a bit more like a bedtime story than an interview, I have no complaint.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked this one. I think the wiki needs more stories like this. No format screw, no needless ambiguity and mystery, just a good story well told.

item.title: Site Criticism Team - Staff Review
item.content:encoded:
This was close to securing my upvote, but there are just parts of this that come off as awkward, forced, or stilted, and they really drew me out of the piece. I'll give some examples, but just know that in general, I dug the idea. I was mostly put off by the execution, hence my downvote.

Some examples of awkward parts:

+ Crit
- Crit

SCP-5063 can not be activated by an individual born after 1975. This is suspected to be due to the popularity of turtle soup in the United States having considerably diminished at that point.

I know you were working with what you were given, but this is an extremely unsatisfying handwave.

Submersion in water limits the trigger effect to physical contact only, unless the water originates from a lake inhabited by alligator snapping turtles, in which case contact between SCP-5063 and the water will activate the effect, transmuting every turtle within the lake into soup.

This just doesn't need to be there at all.

Mr. Edison: Redder didn't show a lot of interest in it, so I simply asked him if I could keep it and he said yes.

It seems unlikely that the character who insisted on using the can to kill nearly all of the local turtles to make his fortune didn't just take the can with him to some other swamp to kill off some other place's turtles.

There were other smaller issues, but those were the examples that tore me out of the piece the most.

Disclaimer: Opinions expressed in this review do not reflect the opinions of SCP Wiki staff as a whole. Please respond to TheMightyMcB or contact the captain of the Site Criticism Team, SoullessSingularity, for any questions or concerns relating to this review.

Bizarre as it may be, I really enjoyed this one. You did an excellent job of extrapolating off of an unusual set of conprocs to work with.

+1 from me

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, I can see where you're coming from, but I intentionally wanted to keep the anomaly simplistic so it wouldn't take focus away from the story and the theme, which is essentially the ecological impact of capitalism, and I also felt like the anomaly, even though simple, served that theme and story well. But yeah, I can totally see that point.

Also yeah, that would be an improvement. I'll make that change when the contest ends.

Also, that video is hilarious.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I do particularly enjoy this story about soup but one thing that I kept on thinking about was that wouldn't this SCP be more suited as an anomalous object? In universe I kinda anticipate that an SCP requires a little bit more kick other than creating soup.

The interview log also feels a little bit cliche (Character want something, someone makes skip to give them that something, it goes wrong) but I can't say I've read this somewhere before.

+ Like literally one nitpick
- only one 

Seeing that this is exquisite corpse I don't know if its very relevant.

Outside of testing, access to SCP-5063 is barred from personnel who do not fulfill either of the following requirements:

Might just be me but its very difficult to understand what this exactly means because of the many negatives. I could suggest:

access to SCP-5063 is barred from personnel who fulfill either of the following requirements

  • Personnel was born before 1975
  • Personnel has been within a 30-meter radius of an alligator snapping turtle (Macrochelys temminckii) within the last 60 days.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Phew, finally it's posted. I'm not gonna lie, this was a bit of a struggle, and I was very unsure of how it was gonna do. However, it's stayed on the plus side so far, so that's enough to make me happy. Also, turtle soup and its history is a surprisingly interesting topic to research.

A big, big thank you to all of my lovely critters: Doctor Dune, Its a Bad Idea, Gamesofmax, Elunerazim, Avelon21, caspian2, JustBixby, Bard Bard, and UncertaintyCrossing. I wouldn't have been able to get this to this state without you all.
